# PANZI — Polyhedral Acoustic Network & Zero-delay Intensity Engine

## What this is
PANZI is a free, open-source (GPL-3.0) multi-channel spatial audio plugin for VST3/AU.
It maps 4D/5D acoustic vector field theory onto a pre-baked coefficient framework —
delivering holographic polyhedral speaker imaging at the CPU footprint of a small
parallel EQ bank.

## How it works
For each output channel in a user-selected polyhedral speaker topology (Diamond,
Cube, Cylinder, Sphere), PANZI computes — once, on a background thread — the exact
time delay, air absorption cutoff, inverse-distance gain, and directional intensity
weight from the virtual source to that speaker. 48 raw floats are stored (8 channels
× 6 values, alignas(64)). The audio thread executes:
  Input → Circular delay → SVF lowpass → RBJ shelf → gain × panWeight → Output N

## The 5D vector field mapping
  Ix, Iy, Iz → dot-product pan weight per speaker (directional intensity flux)
  P          → inverse-distance gain scalar (acoustic pressure law)
  φ          → circular delay + SVF cutoff (time-of-flight + air absorption)

## Architecture
- Zero heap allocation on audio thread (all state pre-allocated in prepareToPlay)
- SPSC lock-free atomic swap for background baker → audio thread handoff
- Simper SVF (trapezoidal) for distance-modulated lowpass — stable under modulation
- RBJ biquad for static boundary reflection shelving
- Inherited from FreeAutoPanner: sine LFO, symmetry warp, equal-power pan law,
  level-follower depth, SmoothedValue ramping

## Author
Gary Doman (GareBear99 / TizWildin)
https://github.com/GareBear99
https://garebear99.github.io/TizWildinEntertainmentHUB/

## Ecosystem
Part of the TizWildin plugin ecosystem (20+ free audio plugins).
Related: FreeEQ8 (parametric EQ), FreeVox8 (spectral vocoder)

## License
GPL-3.0

## Source
https://github.com/GareBear99/PANZI-Panner

## Academic paper
PAPER.md in repo root — 15 references including Spors/Rabenstein/Ahrens (WFS),
Meissner (5D intensity field), Kotus & Kostek, Bilbao, Simper SVF, RBJ cookbook,
ISO 9613-1, ISO 354.

## Version
0.2.0-dev (source complete, binary certification pending)
